file_insights.changes_summary: Added two new model classes: OrderItem and ServiceRequest with comprehensive documentation, fields, properties, and methods
key_additions: OrderItem model with 20+ fields including UUID PK, FK relationships, pricing, status workflow, timestamps, and metadata
ServiceRequest model with priority system, status workflow, and waiter communication features
Both models use SoftDeleteMixin and TimeStampedMixin for soft deletes and audit timestamps
Rich property methods for status checking, formatting, and calculations
Custom managers using SoftDeleteManager for tenant isolation
Comprehensive docstrings with usage examples and workflow diagrams
fields_added: OrderItem: 19 fields including order FK, product FK, variant FK, quantity, unit_price, total_price, currency, status, modifiers JSON, notes, is_gift, timestamps, cancel_reason, metadata
ServiceRequest: 14 fields including order FK, table FK, request_type, priority, status, assigned_waiter FK, timestamps, notes, metadata
methods_added: OrderItem: calculate_total(), set_status(), start_preparation(), mark_ready(), deliver(), cancel(), archive_after_days(), get_preparation_time()
ServiceRequest: assign_to_waiter(), complete(), cancel(), resolve(), mark_acknowledged(), reopen()
indexes_created: OrderItem: composite index on (order, status), (order, deleted_at); single indexes on product, status
ServiceRequest: composite indexes on (order, status), (table, status); single indexes on priority, assigned_waiter

patterns_discovered.description: Both models implement explicit state machines with linear progressions and terminal states. OrderItem: PENDING→PREPARING→READY→DELIVERED, with CANCELLED as terminal. ServiceRequest: PENDING→ACKNOWLEDGED→IN_PROGRESS→RESOLVED with CANCELLED as terminal.
patterns_discovered.relevance: Ensures deterministic state transitions and prevents invalid operations (e.g., marking ready from non-preparing state)

patterns_discovered.description: Both models access organization through parent FK relationships (order.organization). No direct organization_id field, enforcing cascading isolation.
patterns_discovered.relevance: Implements multi-tenancy pattern documented in module docstring; all queries implicitly filtered by organization

patterns_discovered.description: OrderItem captures modifiers and metadata as JSON snapshots at order time rather than maintaining live references. Allows product changes without affecting historical orders.
patterns_discovered.relevance: Decouples order history from product catalog changes; critical for audit trails and repricing protection

patterns_discovered.description: Both models implement SoftDeleteManager as default and models.Manager as all_objects, providing filtered/unfiltered access.
patterns_discovered.relevance: Supports GDPR-style logical deletes while preserving audit capability

patterns_discovered.description: Extensive use of @property decorators for derived values (is_pending, is_ready, formatted_unit_price, display_name, etc.) avoiding code duplication in queries.
patterns_discovered.relevance: Improves API ergonomics and reduces template logic complexity

patterns_discovered.description: Status transitions automatically populate associated timestamp fields (prepared_at on mark_ready, delivered_at on deliver), creating natural audit trail.
patterns_discovered.relevance: Enables analytics queries on preparation times without separate event tables

gotchas_discovered.description: Models override delete() method to raise NotImplementedError, forcing use of soft_delete(). Repeated in docstrings and method signatures.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Prevents accidental hard deletes that would violate audit requirements; developers must use soft_delete() or all_objects.delete()
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Exception handling with clear error message; inline documentation

gotchas_discovered.description: OrderItem.unit_price is captured snapshot at order time, not live reference to Product. If product prices change, historical orders preserve original prices.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Developers might expect unit_price to auto-update; requires database migration and recalculation if pricing strategy changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documented in docstring and help_text; calculate_total() method provided for explicit updates

gotchas_discovered.description: Methods like mark_ready() validate current status and raise ValueError if transition invalid (e.g., can't mark_ready from PENDING). No automatic state coercion.
gotchas_discovered.impact: API calls will fail if client doesn't track state correctly; requires proper error handling in views/serializers
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear error messages; status properties (is_preparing) provided for client-side checks

gotchas_discovered.description: is_gift flag forces total_price to 0 in calculate_total(), but unit_price and modifiers remain non-zero. Affects inventory/COGS calculations.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Gift items show non-zero unit costs but zero total; financial reports must filter on is_gift to properly attribute revenue
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Flag documented with help_text; calculate_total() method shows behavior

gotchas_discovered.description: Both product and variant are nullable FKs with SET_NULL, allowing historical order references even after product soft-deletion. Schema preserves reference but product data may be unavailable.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Queries for deleted product info must check if product is None; property methods fallback to metadata for display
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: metadata JSON field stores snapshots; product_name property handles None gracefully

gotchas_discovered.description: modifier_total property iterates and sums modifiers, each supporting both price and quantity fields. calculate_total() multiplies modifier_total by item quantity, creating nested multiplication.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Developers might miscalculate: (unit_price * qty) + (modifier_price * qty) vs (unit_price * qty) + modifier_price. Order of operations matters for multi-unit items with modifiers.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documented in docstring with explicit formula; property method centralizes calculation

objectives_achieved: Created OrderItem model with 19 fields, status workflow, and 8+ business logic methods
Created ServiceRequest model with priority system and 6+ workflow methods
Implemented soft delete enforcement via SoftDeleteManager and exception handling
Added multi-field indexes for query optimization (order+status, table+status, product, priority)
Documented both models with comprehensive docstrings including workflow diagrams and usage examples
Updated module docstring to reflect completed models instead of TODO items
Imported required choice enums (OrderItemStatus, ServiceRequestStatus, ServiceRequestType)

code_quality_metrics.documentation_coverage: Comprehensive - each model has 100+ line docstrings with workflow diagrams, critical rules, attributes, and usage examples
code_quality_metrics.property_count: 16 properties (13 OrderItem, 3 ServiceRequest) for derived values and checks
code_quality_metrics.database_indexes: 8 indexes total (4 OrderItem, 4 ServiceRequest) covering most query patterns
code_quality_metrics.test_coverage: Not explicitly addressed in this subtask

integration_points: OrderItem.order FK cascades to Order model (parent relationship)
ServiceRequest.order FK cascades to Order model
Both models inherit from TimeStampedMixin (created_at, updated_at) and SoftDeleteMixin (deleted_at, soft_delete)
design_decisions: UUID primary keys chosen for global uniqueness across distributed systems
JSON fields used for modifiers snapshot (flexibility) and metadata (extensibility)
Nullable product/variant FKs with SET_NULL to preserve historical data when menu items deleted
Dual manager pattern to support both filtered (default) and unfiltered (all_objects) queries
Status workflow enforced through ValueError exceptions rather than silent failures
Timestamp automation (prepared_at, delivered_at) integrated into set_status() method

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive test suites for status transition validation (test all invalid transitions raise ValueError), soft delete enforcement, and modifier cost calculations with edge cases (zero modifiers, multi-unit items, gift items)
recommendations.rationale: Complex state machines and cost calculations require exhaustive testing; ValueError exceptions need to be verified; soft delete enforcement is critical for data integrity

recommendations.recommendation: Ensure database migration includes index creation for composite (order, status) and (table, status) queries; consider adding database constraints to enforce status enum values if ORM validation is insufficient
recommendations.rationale: Indexes are critical for kitchen/waiter query performance; database constraints provide defense-in-depth

recommendations.recommendation: Create serializers that expose status properties (is_pending, is_ready) as read-only fields for client-side state checking; use CallableDefaultValidator for status transitions to catch invalid transitions early
recommendations.rationale: Properties provide rich client API; early validation reduces roundtrip errors

recommendations.recommendation: Document that revenue reports must exclude is_gift=True items and handle nullable product FKs gracefully; add metadata.product_name fallback to all product references in analytics
recommendations.rationale: Gift flag behavior and soft-deleted products could cause accounting errors if not handled explicitly

recommendations.recommendation: Create usage guide with examples of: (1) creating order items with modifiers, (2) transitioning ServiceRequests through workflow, (3) querying items by status/priority for kitchen/waiter displays, (4) handling soft deletes in migrations
recommendations.rationale: Complex models with business logic require developer guidance; examples prevent misuse

recommendations.recommendation: Add signal handlers to log status transitions (PENDING→PREPARING, READY→DELIVERED) and cancellations with reasons for operational visibility; log soft_delete calls to detect accidental deletions
recommendations.rationale: Status transitions are business-critical events; audit trail enables troubleshooting and compliance

recommendations.recommendation: Consider denormalizing frequently-accessed fields (order.organization_id, table.organization_id) to OrderItem/ServiceRequest to avoid subqueries in tenant-filtered queries; profile multi-item order queries with select_related/prefetch_related
recommendations.rationale: Tenant isolation via parent FKs may require join overhead; denormalization could improve query performance at scale
